name: Sweet Stuffed Japanese Eggplants
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Spray a 9- by 13-inch baking pan with cooking spray.
3. Make a deep lengthwise slit in each eggplant, but do not cut all the way through. Set aside.
4. To make stuffing, heat oil in a large skillet and saute onion and bell pepper until soft, about 5 minutes.
5. Add remaining ingredients to the skillet, stir, and simmer for 2 minutes.
6. Spread a thin layer of stuffing on the bottom of the baking pan.
7. Spoon the remaining stuffing into each cut of the eggplant and place each stuffed eggplant on top of the stuffing layer in the baking dish.
8. Cover the pan with foil.
9. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 25 minutes.
10. Uncover and bake an additional 20 to 25 minutes, or until the eggplants are tender. Serve over rice. Serves 8.
Per serving: 85 calories; 2 grams protein; 4 grams fat; 12 grams carbohydrate; 0 cholesterol; 10 milligrams sodium; 2 grams fiber; vegan
Source: Vegetarian Times, Jan 94/MM by DEEANN
